artifacts.content:
Hi Elena,

Finally catching my breath long enough to write you a proper update. Things here at Pacific Vista (Unit 602, Building B) have felt like one long medical/property-management mashup.

Nanay Loling has been staying with us full-time in 602 since her mild stroke back in March 2024, so we can keep an eye on her blood pressure and do the home PT exercises. Now it’s gotten more complicated: GMH started her on home dialysis through their outpatient program, and her nephrologist there told us on January 7 that any lead or copper in the condo’s tap water could make her kidney failure worse. Between that and her diabetes/BP meds, I’m watching every drop she drinks.

You remember the small kitchen fire we had here in B‑602 on November 18, 2023? We finally closed out the $7,800 smoke-damage claim on the cabinets and ceiling, but I still get nervous every time someone plugs in more than one thing at that outlet. Add in Carlos couch‑surfing on our sofa since his DUI arrest in Dededo in May 2023 while he finishes court-ordered counseling, and the place feels like a halfway house some days.

Money’s tight but manageable. We fell a couple months behind on the Pacific Vista HOA dues last January and cut out bottled water to catch up, so we’ve been filling jugs straight from the Unit 602 kitchen cold tap. Now I’m running numbers to see if we can squeeze out another $120/month for bottled just for Nanay if the lab says the GWA water isn’t safe for her dialysis.

Kids are okay—Lucas and Mia still at San Vicente, same morning chaos. Sundays we still try to make it to Mass in Hagåtña, then over to Carlos’s place in Dededo when he’s actually there.

How are things in San Diego? Write when you can.

Love,
Dan

Hi Ang,

Just got back upstairs and wanted to put this all in one place before the day runs away.

I did the three water samples this morning like we talked about. First one (PV‑01) was from our Unit 602 kitchen cold tap at 6:45 a.m., first‑draw after the water sat overnight. That’s the same tap we’ve been using every school morning since August 2023 to mix Lucas’s ADHD meds before we drive him to San Vicente, and the one we switched to for all our drinking water last January when we were two months behind on the HOA dues and stopped buying bottled.

I keep thinking about when Carlos replaced that faucet on April 3, 2024 after Nanay’s rash and the dermatologist at Guam Regional hinted metals in the water might be aggravating it—and how he also rinsed paint thinner off his hands in that same sink after the moldy drywall job in the 6th‑floor hallway on March 16, 2024. Not exactly reassuring.

Second sample (PV‑02) was the lobby fountain by the mailboxes across from the HOA office. That’s the one Lucas uses after his Tuesday/Thursday speech therapy with Ms. Regina Cruz in 305 and where Nanay fills her pill bottles with water around 6:30 a.m. before her diabetes and BP meds. It’s also the same fountain that set off Lucas’s migraine/asthma combo last April 18, 2025 before we ended up at Guam Regional’s ER.

Third (PV‑03) was the fitness center restroom sink on the second floor—the mid‑building tap near where I met with Marissa on August 14 about the mold/sewer smell complaints, and the same sink Lucas used on October 3 before that rash the peds doctor at Guam Regional thought might be from bacteria.

I dropped everything at the WERI lab by 9:15 and paid the $185 with HOA check 104982. Regular 7‑day turnaround, so we should know something before the board budget meeting.

Heading back down to walk the pool deck. Text if you need anything.

Love,
D

metadata.subject: This morning’s water samples (for Nanay & the kids)

artifacts.content:
Hi Carlos,

Checking in on you and also wanted to loop you in on the water situation here at Pacific Vista.

WERI’s running a full potability panel on three treated drinking water samples from the building. I collected them myself on January 15 between 6:45–7:55 a.m. from (1) our Unit 602 kitchen tap, (2) the ground floor lobby fountain, and (3) the second‑floor fitness center restroom sink, then drove them over and relinquished custody at about 9:15. HOA is covering the $185 (check #104982) under PO‑2026‑0173 tied to the plumbing upgrades for BP‑23‑1047.

I pushed for this after my own blood work at FHP last June 3 came back with a lead level of 5.4 µg/dL. Dr. Manuel Santos at the FHP Family Clinic told me on June 10 to get the condo’s water tested before we start putting Lucas and Mia through more blood draws. Between that, Lucas’s ER trip to Guam Regional on April 18, 2025 after using the lobby fountain, and Nanay’s new dialysis at GMH, I’m not taking chances.

I keep remembering all the times you’ve helped here—replacing the 602 kitchen faucet on April 3, 2024 after Nanay’s rash, doing those unpermitted after‑hours mold repairs in the 6th‑floor hallway, and now the weekly Friday flushes you’ve been doing at the lobby fountain since Guam EPA’s informal warning about low chlorine last May. I’m grateful, but I also worry we’re patching a bigger problem with band‑aids.

How’s the counseling going since the DUI in Dededo? If you ever want to crash here again for a bit, you know the couch is open (as long as you don’t rinse paint thinner or blood down my sinks anymore).

Let’s catch a game soon—maybe after Sunday Mass and lunch at your place in Dededo.

Dan

metadata.subject: Water tests, health stuff, and checking on you

artifacts.content:
Title: Water/health running list – for my own sanity

1. Household
- Address: Pacific Vista Condominiums, 1840 Marine View Drive, Unit 602, Tamuning, GU 96913.
- Residents: me, Angela, kids (Lucas 11, Mia 8), Nanay Dolores “Loling” Santos (here full‑time since mild stroke March 2024), plus Carlos on and off since his May 2023 DUI in Dededo while he finishes court‑ordered counseling.
- Kids alone 6th floor stack most weekdays 3:00–5:30 p.m. since August 2024 until Angela gets off her Guam Memorial Hospital shift. Need to remind them about locking door/no stove.

2. Medical triggers tied to water
- My blood lead: 5.4 µg/dL (FHP lab, 3 June 2025). Dr. Manuel Santos said on 10 June to test condo water before more invasive tests on kids.
- Nanay: GMH outpatient dialysis; nephrologist warned 7 Jan 2026 about lead/copper in GWA tap water. Also diabetes/BP meds—she fills pill bottles at lobby fountain ~6:30 a.m.
- Lucas: ER at Guam Regional Medical City 18 April 2025 (asthma + migraine) after drinking from lobby fountain post‑basketball. Rash on arms after rinsing at fitness center restroom sink 3 Oct 2024; peds doc worried about bacteria.
- Teresa Cruz (Unit 305): 2 Sept 2025 complaint, rash allegedly from metallic/discolored water at fitness center restroom; dermatologist at FHP linked to possible contaminants; attorney pushing HOA liability claim.

3. Incidents/other
- Small kitchen fire, B‑602, 18 Nov 2023 from overloaded outlet; $7,800 smoke‑damage insurance claim.
- Used syringe found June 27, 2024 in gym restroom stall near PV‑03; tied to unapproved Airbnb renter in 304.
- Island Mechanical tech cut hand 9 Sept 2024; rinsed blood in same PV‑03 sink during leak repair.

4. To‑do
- Follow up with WERI when results ready.
- Meet HOA attorney about Teresa claim + ex‑207 owner civil complaint re “water made her sick” (served at lobby fountain 3 Nov 2024).
- Talk to Angela about budgeting $120/month bottled water if needed for Nanay.

Dear Board Members,

I wanted to provide a more complete narrative tying together recent water‑related concerns at Pacific Vista so you have context before our operating budget discussion.

As you know, our current project under building permit BP‑23‑1047 includes plumbing upgrades. In support of that, and in light of several health complaints, I delivered three treated drinking water samples to WERI on January 15 (regular 7‑working‑day turnaround). The HOA is paying $185 by check #104982 under PO‑2026‑0173.

The sampling locations were chosen based on actual resident use:

1) PV‑01 – Unit 602 kitchen cold tap (sixth floor, ocean‑facing stack). This is my residence and also the unit where my mother‑in‑law, Dolores “Loling” Santos, has lived full‑time since her mild stroke in March 2024. Her GMH nephrologist recently warned that any lead/copper in our tap water could worsen her kidney failure. It’s also the tap we’ve used for my son Lucas’s ADHD medication every school morning since August 2023 and our main drinking source since January 2024 when my family briefly fell two months behind on HOA dues and stopped buying bottled water.

2) PV‑02 – Ground floor lobby fountain across from the HOA office/mailboxes. Several written complaints since March 2025 cite a strong chlorine/metallic odor. My son had an asthma/migraine episode after using this fountain on April 18, 2025 and was treated at Guam Regional Medical City ER. My mother‑in‑law also uses this fountain daily around 6:30 a.m. to fill pill bottles for her diabetes and blood pressure meds.

3) PV‑03 – Second‑floor fitness center restroom sink (mid‑building). This is the fixture tied to Ms. Teresa Cruz’s September 2, 2025 rash complaint and liability claim, as well as a June 27, 2024 used‑syringe incident and a September 9, 2024 contractor injury where blood was rinsed into the basin.

Once WERI’s report is in, I recommend we schedule a special meeting to review any exceedances and discuss options (flushing, pipe inspection, possible riser replacement) before final permit clearance.

Respectfully,

Daniel K. Herrera
Property Manager
Pacific Vista Condominiums HOA

metadata.subject: Context for water testing and resident health complaints

artifacts.content:
Event: WERI water results + medical follow‑ups

Date: 2026-01-24
Time: 3:00–4:30 p.m.
Location: Unit 602 / phone
Attendees: Daniel, Angela (loop in Dr. Santos/GMH nephrologist by phone later)

Description:

Block time to (1) review WERI’s drinking water report for Pacific Vista (PV‑01, PV‑02, PV‑03; treated GWA water, regular 7‑day TAT) and (2) plan next steps for family health and HOA.

Talking points:

Home/family
- Nanay Dolores “Loling” Santos – living with us full‑time in Unit 602 since March 2024 after mild stroke. Started GMH outpatient/home dialysis this month. Her nephrologist warned on 7 Jan 2026 about lead/copper in our tap water. If any exceedances: budget ~$120/month for bottled water for her meds/dialysis. Decide whether she should stop using lobby fountain by mailboxes at 6:30 a.m. for pill bottles.
- My labs – 5.4 µg/dL blood lead from FHP on 3 June 2025; Dr. Manuel Santos advised on 10 June to test condo water before more invasive tests on Lucas and Mia. If WERI flags issues, schedule pediatric labs.
- Lucas – review April 18, 2025 ER visit at Guam Regional Medical City (asthma/migraine after lobby fountain) and October 3, 2024 rash after rinsing at fitness center restroom sink; also consider that we’ve been mixing his ADHD meds with 602 kitchen tap since August 2023.

HOA / risk
- Teresa Cruz (Unit 305) rash complaint 2 Sept 2025; dermatologist at FHP suspected contaminants from fitness center restroom sink.
- Used syringe in gym restroom stall 27 June 2024; Island Mechanical tech blood rinse 9 Sept 2024.
- Ex‑Unit 207 owner civil complaint served 3 Nov 2024 by lobby fountain claiming building water made her sick.

Outcome: list of questions for Dr. Santos and GMH nephrologist; draft update for HOA board before next BP‑23‑1047 check‑in.

Hi Atty. Santos,

Following up on our last call about the various water‑related issues at Pacific Vista.

From a risk perspective, the second‑floor fitness center restroom (PV‑03) worries me the most. That single cold tap has now been tied to:

• Ms. Teresa Cruz in Unit 305 – her September 2, 2025 written complaint and subsequent liability claim, alleging that the intermittent metallic taste and morning discoloration in that restroom aggravated her pre‑existing skin condition. Her dermatologist at FHP Health Center reportedly linked the rash to possible water contaminants and her attorney specifically asked that I document any lab results.

• My son Lucas – he rinsed off in that same sink on October 3, 2024 before developing a rash on his arms; his pediatrician at Guam Regional Medical City raised concerns about bacteria in the gym restroom fixtures.

• My mother‑in‑law, Dolores “Loling” Santos – during May 2024, she used that restroom to rinse her dialysis port bandages after PT at Guam Memorial Hospital, before she was hospitalized for a bloodstream infection.

• Two separate incident reports: (1) June 27, 2024 – I found a used syringe in the stall near that sink after security reported suspected drug use by a short‑term renter in Unit 304 (unapproved Airbnb); (2) September 9, 2024 – an Island Mechanical technician cut his hand while repairing a leaking supply line above the gym ceiling and rinsed blood down that faucet.

I’ve already arranged for WERI to run a standard treated drinking water panel on PV‑03 as part of our January 15 sampling. Once we have results, I’d appreciate your guidance on how much detail to share with residents and whether we should temporarily restrict access to the fitness center restroom while we sort out any remediation.

Separately, we still need to address the ex‑Unit 207 owner’s civil complaint served at the lobby fountain on November 3, 2024 alleging the building’s water made her sick.

Best,

Daniel K. Herrera
Property Manager
Pacific Vista Condominiums HOA
